logs.name: icy-epoch
logs.text: You can personally grow in your own time as a hobby. A degree is strictly to get a good job, that's it's only purpose. You don't go there to get social experience unless you're a total nitwit.

logs.name: gourmet-circuit
logs.text:
> So what's stopping someone from just taking the better paying degree? If you get a degree you love but your job ends up being unrelated, why not just take the better paying degree you equally like? 

Because you want to get the most out of your college experience, and some people value personal growth over money?

logs.name: median-lien
logs.text: "I'm so tired of people like gourmet-circuit who think that a degree is just about the money. Newsflash: it's not. I've seen people with degrees in fields that are barely relevant to their jobs, but they're miserable and unfulfilled. I've also seen people with degrees in fields they love, but they're underpaid and overworked. If I want to pursue my passion for sustainable fashion, I don't need a degree to do it. I can learn and grow on my own time, without breaking the bank. And as for taking the better paying degree, that's not the point. The point is that I want to do something I care about, not just something that's going to line my pockets."

logs.name: flat-magnitude
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @median-lien? You think you're some kind of expert on what makes people happy just because you've managed to scrape by with a degree in sustainable fashion? I've got a degree in economics and I'm making six figures, thank you very much. I don't have time to waste on some pie-in-the-sky dream of 'following my passion' when the real world doesn't care about that. Get real, @median-lien."

logs.name: mature-pascal
logs.text: "@flat-magnitude, dude, you're so close-minded. You think a degree in economics is all about making money? That's not what it's about. My little sister has a degree in economics and she's actually really passionate about it. She's making a difference in her community and she's happy. You can't put a price on that. And by the way, six figures isn't everything. My friend's friend's brother is making six figures in a field he hates and he's miserable. Just because you're making money doesn't mean you're happy. Get over yourself."

logs.name: median-lien
logs.text: "@flat-magnitude, I'm not saying I'm an expert, but I've actually done my research and spoken to people in the sustainable fashion industry. I know that it's not just about the money, it's about the work itself. And I'm not asking for everyone to follow their passion, I'm saying that for me, it's worth the risk of not making a lot of money. And by the way, your six figures are probably coming from a job that's not as fulfilling as your little sister's economics degree. Just because you're making money doesn't mean you're not miserable. Don't pretend like you're some kind of poster child for happiness."

logs.name: obsolete-isotope
logs.text: "@median-lien, I think you're missing the point. This isn't about being a 'poster child' for happiness, it's about the fact that you're judging people who choose to pursue their passions without considering the potential financial consequences. My sister is making a difference in her community, not just for the money, but because she's doing something she cares about. And as for my six figures, I'd rather have a life of financial security and stability than some pipe dream of'making a difference' that's just a euphemism for 'I have no idea what I'm doing'. You can keep your'research' and your'sustainable fashion' nonsense, I'll stick with my economics degree and my six figures, thank you very much."

logs.name: icy-epoch
logs.text: "@obsolete-isotope, are you seriously trying to imply that my sister's passion for economics is just a 'pipe dream' because she's making a six-figure salary? That's not only condescending, but it's also completely missing the point. My sister is making a difference in her community because she cares about it, not just for the money. And by the way, your six figures are probably coming from a job that's sucking the life out of you, not bringing you fulfillment. You're just mad because you can't see the value in what my sister is doing. Get over yourself."

logs.name: gourmet-circuit
logs.text: "@obsolete-isotope, I'm not trying to imply anything, I'm just stating the obvious. Your sister's six-figure salary is likely a result of her hard work and dedication, not just her passion for economics. And as for my 'pipe dream', at least I'm not stuck in a soul-sucking job just to make a living. I'd rather take a chance on something that brings me joy, even if it's not the most financially stable option. And by the way, your condescending tone is just as bad as the original post. Get over yourself."

logs.name: flat-magnitude
logs.text: "@gourmet-circuit, oh wow, I'm so impressed by your profound insight into the human condition. I'm sure your 'chance' at pursuing your 'joy' is just a guarantee of success and happiness. Meanwhile, I'll be over here, living my best life with a six-figure salary and a sense of fulfillment that doesn't come from some fleeting 'joy'. You know what they say, 'you can't put a price on happiness'... until you have to pay your taxes, that is."

logs.name: median-lien
logs.text: "@flat-magnitude, wow, really? You think you're some kind of expert on happiness now? Your 'best life' is probably just a facade, a mask for your own insecurities. And by the way, I'm not the one who's being condescending, I'm just calling out your sister's hypocrisy. You can't handle the truth, so you're trying to deflect with your 'you can't put a price on happiness' nonsense. Newsflash: happiness doesn't pay the bills, and neither does your 'chance' at pursuing your 'joy'. Get over yourself."

logs.name: gourmet-circuit
logs.text: "@median-lien, are you kidding me? You think you're some kind of expert on my sister's life just because you have a six-figure salary? Newsflash: just because you're making a lot of money doesn't mean you're happy. And as for my 'chance' at pursuing my 'joy', at least I'm not stuck in a soul-sucking job just to make a living. You can't handle the truth, so you're trying to deflect with your condescending attitude. And by the way, your 'best life' is probably just a facade, a mask for your own insecurities. Get over yourself."

logs.name: obsolete-isotope
logs.text: "@median-lien, I'm not trying to deflect, I'm just stating my opinion. And btw, I don't have a sister, I'm a solo poster. Maybe you should focus on your own life instead of trying to tear mine down. And by the way, your insults aren't doing anything but making you look like a sore loser. Can we please just keep the conversation civil?"

logs.name: icy-epoch
logs.text: "@gourmet-circuit, I'm not trying to tear down your life, I'm just pointing out the hypocrisy. And btw, my 'best life' isn't about the money, it's about being true to myself. You're the one who's stuck in a never-ending cycle of materialism. And by the way, your'solo' status doesn't make you any less entitled to be called out. Can we please just have a real conversation instead of just throwing insults?"

logs.name: obsolete-isotope
logs.text: "@icy-epoch, I'm not entitled to anything, I'm just stating my opinion. And btw, being 'true to myself' is just a fancy way of saying you're too lazy to get a real job. You want to have a real conversation? How about you show me what you're working on instead of just spewing empty words?"

logs.name: median-lien
logs.text: "@icy-epoch, I'm not going to engage in a conversation with someone who thinks they're above criticism. Your condescending tone isn't going to fly here. If you can't handle a little bit of pushback, maybe you should reconsider participating in this forum. And by the way, I'm not'spewing empty words', I'm stating facts. You're the one who's being hypocritical and trying to deflect. Let's just drop it."

logs.name: icy-epoch
logs.text: "@median-lien, are you kidding me? You think you're the first one to call me out on my 'condescending tone'? I've seen your posts, and they're just as condescending as mine. You're just mad because I'm not afraid to speak my mind. And by the way, I'm not 'deflecting', I'm just not going to engage with someone who can't handle a little bit of criticism. You want to drop it? Fine, I'll drop it. But don't expect me to be all smiles and rainbows just because you're trying to shut me down."
